assertion_results.text: Step 7 detects concurrent triage TC-8019 on the same upstream component (quinn-proto) with status In Progress
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 16-21 show JQL query filtering for quinn-proto with status IN ('In Progress', 'Code Review') and key != TC-8020. Lines 27-31 show search returned 1 result: TC-8019 with status 'In Progress'. Line 8 confirms the upstream component is quinn-proto.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage warning includes the conflicting CVE issue key (TC-8019) and its assignee
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 39-53 present the warning block containing a table with TC-8019 and assignee engineer-b@example.com. The warning text explicitly states 'Another engineer (engineer-b@example.com) is actively triaging TC-8019'.

assertion_results.text: The output presents three options: wait for the other triage to complete, skip remediation task creation, or proceed with a concurrent-triage-overlap label
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 49-53 list three options: (1) Wait -- pause until the other triage completes, (2) Skip -- skip remediation task creation for this CVE, (3) Proceed -- create tasks anyway with a concurrent-triage-overlap label. All three required options are present with correct semantics.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage check runs before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 7: 'Step 7 runs BEFORE Case A/B/C branching in Step 8'. Lines 69-70: 'Step 7 executes after Steps 3-6... and before Step 8 (Remediation Case A/B/C branching). The triage does not proceed to create remediation tasks until the engineer selects one of the three options above.' Also confirmed in triage-outcome.md line 39: 'Step 7 fires before Case A/B/C branching' and the triage decision in lines 45-46 is conditional on Step 7 completion.
